---
title: "SHM Worked Example 5.16"
subtitle: "Reference: Applied Mechanics: Reed's Vol. 2"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
---

**Ex. 5.16**: A machine component of mass $m = 2.25$ kg moves with simple harmonic motion.
The amplitude is $A = 380$ mm, and the frequency of oscillation is 120 oscillations per minute.
Determine:

1. The maximum accelerating force.
2. The accelerating force when the displacement is 250 mm from the mean position.

## Given Data

- Mass, $m = 2.25$ kg
- Amplitude, $A = 380$ mm $= 0.38$ m
- Frequency, $f = \dfrac{120}{60} = 2$ Hz

The angular velocity of the motion is given by:

$$\omega = 2\pi f = 2\pi \times 2 = 4\pi = 12.5664 \ \text{rad/s}$$

## (i) Maximum Accelerating Force

The maximum acceleration occurs at maximum displacement ($x = A$) and is given by:

$$a_{\max} = \omega^2 A$$

Substituting the values:

$$a_{\max} = (4\pi)^2 \times 0.38 = 16\pi^2 \times 0.38 = 60.0072 \ \text{m/s}^2$$

The maximum accelerating force is:

$$F_{\max} = m \, a_{\max} = 2.25 \times 60.0072 = 135.0162 \ \text{N}$$

## (ii) Accelerating Force at $x = 250$ mm

The acceleration at displacement $x$ is given by:

$$a = \omega^2 x$$

Substituting $x = 250$ mm $= 0.25$ m:

$$a = (4\pi)^2 \times 0.25 = 16\pi^2 \times 0.25 = 39.4784 \ \text{m/s}^2$$

The accelerating force is:

$$F = m \, a = 2.25 \times 39.4784 = 88.8264 \ \text{N}$$

## Final Answers

1. Maximum accelerating force: $\boldsymbol{F_{\max} = 135.0162}$ **N**
2. Accelerating force at $x = 250$ mm: $\boldsymbol{F = 88.8264}$ **N**
